title: Projection system with enhanced color and contrast
abstract: A system can include a projection layer. The projection layer can include a plurality of pixels. The plurality of pixels can be electronically controllable to vary appearance of at least one of the plurality of pixels in coordination with an image projected onto the projection layer.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A system, comprising: a projection layer including a plurality of pixels, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable to vary appearance of at least one of the plurality of pixels in coordination with an image projected onto the projection layer. The system of claim 1, wherein the projection layer displays the image in synchronization and alignment with the image projected onto the projection layer. The system of claim 1, wherein the projection layer comprises at least one non-transparent display. The system of claim 1, wherein the projection layer comprises at least one transparent display. The system of claim 4, wherein the transparent display is non-emissive. The system of claim5, wherein the non-emissive display includes at least one pixel of the plurality of pixels that includes dye. The system of claim 5, wherein the non-emissive display includes at least one pixel of the plurality of pixels that does not include dye and appears substantially white. The system of claim 1, wherein the projection layer includes a plurality of non-emissive and transparent displays. The system of claim 1, wherein the projection layer includes at least one of a first sensor configured to detect light, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the light detected by the first sensor, a second sensor configured to detect distance of a user relative to the projection layer, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the distance detected by the second sensor and a third sensor configured to detect an attribute of a user within a predetermined distance of the projection layer, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the attribute of the user detected by the third sensor. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a projector configured to project the image onto the projection layer. The system of claim 10, wherein the projector includes a camera configured to capture image data of the image projected on the projection layer, wherein the projector is configured to adjust the image projected on the projection layer based upon the image data from the camera. A method, comprising: providing a projection layer including a plurality of pixels, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable to vary appearance of at least one of the plurality of pixels in coordination with an image projected onto the projection layer, wherein the projection layer displays the image in synchronization and alignment with the image projected onto the projection layer. The method of claim 12, wherein the projection layer includes a least one of a first sensor configured to detect light, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the light detected by the first sensor, a second sensor configured to detect distance of a user relative to the projection layer, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the distance detected by the second sensor and a third sensor configured to detect an attribute of a user within a predetermined distance of the projection layer, wherein the plurality of pixels is electronically controllable based, at least in part, upon the attribute of the user detected by the third sensor. The method of claim 12, further comprising: providing a projector configured to project the image onto the projection layer. The method of claim 14, further comprising: providing a camera configured to capture image data of the image projected on the projection layer, wherein the projector is configured to adjust the image projected on the projection layer based upon the image data from the camera.
